# AI Trading Bot
> An independent, non-custodial dashboard for understanding AI-assisted crypto trading signals, routes, risks, and user-approved execution without surrendering private keys.

## What is AI Trading Bot
An AI trading bot is software that turns market data, strategy rules, and model outputs into signals or trade instructions. It may use machine learning, statistical models, natural-language analysis, or ordinary rule-based logic marketed as AI. Signal dashboards inform users; execution bots also submit orders through exchange APIs or blockchain transactions. This dashboard emphasizes DeFi previews and wallet-approved actions rather than custody or guaranteed automation.

## How it works
1. The user selects a market, asset pair, chain, strategy conditions, and risk limits.
2. The system reads price, liquidity, order-book, or on-chain data and applies its rules or model.
3. Risk controls evaluate position size, slippage, liquidity, and exit conditions before presenting a signal.
4. The dashboard shows the proposed route, quote, assumptions, and required approval.
5. Where execution is available, the user signs with a wallet; the dashboard cannot sign without the user’s authorization.

## Key facts
- Common types are signal-only, human-approved, and fully automated bots.
- “AI” describes a technique, not a verified performance or safety standard.
- The category has no universal token, contract, blockchain, or official protocol.
- EVM networks use EIP-155 chain IDs; Ethereum mainnet is chain ID `1`, represented as `eip155:1` under CAIP-2.
- ERC-20 approvals can authorize a contract to transfer tokens up to a stated allowance.
- Centralized-exchange bots use API credentials; permissions determine whether they can view data, trade, or withdraw.
- A non-custodial design keeps private keys with the user, but signatures, allowances, and smart contracts still create risk.
- Signal generation may be immediate, while execution depends on venue latency, liquidity, congestion, and blockchain confirmation.
- Real costs can include subscriptions, maker/taker fees, spreads, slippage, gas, and failed-transaction fees.

## Common questions

### Is an AI trading bot safe?
No bot removes market or operational risk. Verify data sources, permissions, contracts, and every wallet request; guaranteed-return claims are a fraud warning.

### Does the dashboard hold my crypto?
No. Its non-custodial flow does not require private-key handoff or a deposit, and the wallet remains the final authorization point.

### What fees apply?
Viewing information does not itself create a blockchain fee. A completed trade may incur venue fees, spread, slippage, gas, and any app pricing disclosed before action.

### How long does setup or execution take?
Setup depends on the strategy, chain, and wallet flow. Exchange execution is venue-dependent; on-chain settlement requires transaction inclusion and the chosen confirmation level.

### Which assets and chains are supported?
There is no universal support list for AI bots. This dashboard describes ETH and BTC-linked markets; rely on the live app for the currently available networks, assets, and routes.

### How should a beginner start?
Begin with read-only analysis or paper trading, define loss and exposure limits, and review results after fees. If enabling execution later, use restricted API permissions or a separate wallet with limited funds and allowances.

### What are the main risks?
Market loss, bad data, model error, overfitting, outages, compromised credentials, malicious approvals, smart-contract bugs, illiquidity, slippage, oracle failure, and MEV can all affect outcomes.

## App
- Dashboard: https://ai-trading-bot-web.github.io/
- App: https://ai-trading-bot-web.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
CFTC AI Trading Bots Advisory: https://www.cftc.gov/PressRoom/PressReleases/8854-24
SEC Investor Alert on AI and Investment Fraud: https://www.investor.gov/introduction-investing/general-resources/news-alerts/alerts-bulletins/investor-alerts/artificial-intelligence-fraud
Coinbase Advanced Trade API: https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/api-reference/advanced-trade-api/rest-api/introduction
ERC-20 Token Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-20
Ethereum Provider API: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1193
Sign-In with Ethereum: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-4361
CAIP-2 Blockchain IDs: https://standards.chainagnostic.org/CAIPs/caip-2
NIST AI Risk Management Framework: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework

## Data & references
CoinGecko Market Data API: https://www.coingecko.com/en/api
DefiLlama API: https://api-docs.defillama.com/
Ethereum Explorer: https://etherscan.io/
Solana Explorer: https://solscan.io/
Automated Trading System Overview: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automated_trading_system
